title: अश्विनी कुमार स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अश्विनी कुमार स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ अश्विनौ देवौ भिषजौ सुहस्तौ
तावस्मभ्यं शर्म यच्छन्तु शन्तमम्।
भिषक्तमौ विश्वस्य भेषजौ
नासत्यादस्रौ सरस्वत्यामधि॥१॥

नमोऽश्विभ्यां देवेभ्यो भिषग्भ्यां दिव्यवैद्ययोः।
सुरूपौ सूर्यपुत्रौ च नासत्यौ च प्रपूजयेत्॥२॥

देवानां भिषजौ श्रेष्ठौ स्वर्गलोकमनामयम्।
युवानौ रूपसम्पन्नौ सर्वरोगहरौ शुभौ॥३॥

च्यवनं यौ समुद्धृत्य यौवनं ददतुर्मुनेः।
तावश्विनौ नमस्यामि भिषजौ सर्वदेहिनाम्॥४॥

प्रातः सवने माध्यन्दिने च तृतीयसवने तथा।
सोमपानेन तुष्टौ तावश्विनौ मे प्रसीदताम्॥५॥

मधुविद्यां ददतुर्यौ दध्यञ्चे ऋषिसत्तमे।
तौ दिव्यज्ञानदातारावश्विनौ प्रणमाम्यहम्॥६॥

सन्ध्यायामश्विनौ देवौ प्रभातसमयप्रियौ।
आरोग्यं बलमायुश्च ददतां मम सर्वदा॥७॥

रथेन हिरण्मयेन सूर्यपुत्रौ दिवस्करौ।
आगच्छतमशेषार्तिहरणाय नमो नमः॥८॥

वीर्यवन्तौ दानशीलौ शूरौ देवप्रियौ सदा।
कृपालू भक्तवत्सलौ अश्विनौ मां प्रपालयताम्॥९॥

नासत्यं चैव दस्रं च स्तुत्वा भक्त्या नरो यदि।
सर्वरोगविनिर्मुक्तः सुखमायुश्च विन्दति॥१०॥

॥ इति श्री अश्विनी कुमार स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shrī Aśvinī Kumāra Stotram ||

Om Aśvinau devau bhiṣajau suhastau
Tāvasmabhyaṁ śarma yachchhantu śantamam.
Bhiṣaktamau viśvasya bheṣajau
Nāsatyādasrau Sarasvatyāmadhi.||1||

Namo'śvibhyāṁ devebhyo bhiṣagbhyāṁ divyavaidyayoḥ.
Surūpau Sūryaputrau cha Nāsatyau cha prapūjayet.||2||

Devānāṁ bhiṣajau śreṣṭhau svargalokaamanāmayam.
Yuvānau rūpasampannau sarvarogaharau śubhau.||3||

Chyavanaṁ yau samuddhṛtya yauvanaṁ dadaturmuneḥ.
Tāvaśvinau namasyāmi bhiṣajau sarvadehinām.||4||

Prātaḥ savane mādhyandine cha tṛtīyasavane tathā.
Somapānena tuṣṭau tāvaśvinau me prasīdatām.||5||

Madhuvidyāṁ dadaturyau Dadhyañce ṛṣisattame.
Tau divyajñānadātārāvaśvinau praṇamāmyaham.||6||

Sandhyāyāmaśvinau devau prabhātasamayapriyau.
Ārogyaṁ balamāyuścha dadatāṁ mama sarvadā.||7||

Rathena hiraṇmayena Sūryaputrau divāskarau.
Āgachchhatamaśeṣārtihaaraṇāya namo namaḥ.||8||

Vīryavantau dānaśīlau śūrau devapriyau sadā.
Kṛpālū bhaktavatsalau Aśvinau māṁ prapālayatām.||9||

Nāsatyaṁ chaiva Dasraṁ cha stutvā bhaktyā naro yadi.
Sarvarogavinirmuktaḥ sukhamāyuścha vindati.||10||

|| Iti Shrī Aśvinī Kumāra Stotraṁ sampūrṇam ||
hindiMeaning: (1) अश्विनी कुमार देव, भिषज (चिकित्सक), सुन्दर हाथों वाले हैं - वे हमें सर्वोत्तम शान्ति प्रदान करें। वे विश्व के श्रेष्ठ चिकित्सक, औषधि स्वरूप, नासत्य और दस्र नाम वाले हैं। (2) दिव्य वैद्य, सुरूप, सूर्यपुत्र, नासत्य अश्विनीकुमारों को नमस्कार और पूजा। (3) देवताओं के श्रेष्ठ चिकित्सक, स्वर्गलोक को निरोग करने वाले, युवा, रूपसम्पन्न, सर्वरोगहर, शुभ। (4) जिन्होंने च्यवन ऋषि को उद्धार कर यौवन प्रदान किया, उन सभी प्राणियों के चिकित्सक अश्विनीकुमारों को नमस्कार। (5) प्रातः, मध्यान्ह और तृतीय सवन में सोमपान से सन्तुष्ट अश्विनीकुमार प्रसन्न हों। (6) जिन्होंने दध्यञ्च ऋषि को मधुविद्या (दिव्य ज्ञान) दी, उन दिव्यज्ञानदाता अश्विनीकुमारों को प्रणाम। (7-10) सन्ध्या के देव, प्रभात प्रिय अश्विनीकुमार सदा आरोग्य, बल और आयु दें। नासत्य और दस्र की भक्ति से स्तुति करने वाला सभी रोगों से मुक्त होकर सुख और आयु पाता है।
benefits: Invokes the divine twin physicians for all types of healing
Invokes youth and vitality as they restored chyavana rishi's youth
Invokes the blessing of madhu vidya (divine knowledge of healing)
Especially effective when recited at dawn (Prabhata Samaya)
Freedom from all diseases through the celestial doctors' grace
description: Ashwini Kumara Stotram (अश्विनी कुमार स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise dedicated to Lord Vishnu. Drawn from Rigveda / Puranic Compilation, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to provide health, strength, and longevity, grant youth and vitality as they restored Chyavana Rishi's youth, and bestow Madhu Vidya (divine knowledge of healing). This sacred text is ideally recited during Dhanteras for enhanced blessings. The text contains 10 verses and takes around 6 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Ashwini Kumara Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Ashwini Kumara Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Prayer to the divine twin physicians for healing, health, and rejuvenation.

seoFaqs.question: Who are the Ashwini Kumaras and what is the Ashwini Kumara Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ashwini Kumaras (अश्विनी कुमार) — also called Ashvinau, Nasatyas, Dasras — are the divine twin physician-deities of the Vedic pantheon, the celestial sons of Surya and Sanjna (in her mare-form, hence ashvini = horse-formed) born on the slopes of the Sarasvati. The Ashwini Kumara Stotram (अश्विनी कुमार स्तोत्रम्) is a Sanskrit devotional hymn praising them as devanam bhishajau shreshthau — the foremost physicians of the gods — bestowing health, youth, beauty, longevity and freedom from all diseases (sarva-roga-harau shubhau). The opening verse Om ashvinau devau bhishajau suhastau, tav-asmabhyam sharma yacchhantu shantamam echoes the Rigvedic prayer for healing peace. Recited daily by Ayurvedic vaidyas, students of medicine, and persons seeking healing, it is one of the most ancient health-and-rejuvenation prayers of the Vedic tradition. (Sources: Rigveda; Skanda Purana; Bhavishya Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Vedic origin of the Ashwini Kumaras?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ashwini Kumaras are among the most ancient Vedic devatas, with over 50 hymns of the Rigveda addressed to them. Their origin myth: Sanjna, daughter of Vishvakarma and wife of Surya, unable to bear her husband's blazing radiance, withdrew leaving her shadow Chhaya in her place, and herself took the form of a mare to perform tapas in the northern lands. Surya, discovering this, took the form of a stallion and joined her — and from that union were born the twin Ashwini Kumaras (their name from ashvini = mare-formed). Endowed with youth, beauty and divine medical knowledge, they were appointed by Brahma as the physicians of the gods (deva-vaidya). Their classic exploit was the rejuvenation of the aged sage Chyavana — making him young and handsome again so he could remain married to the young princess Sukanya. (Sources: Rigveda; Mahabharata Vana Parva Chyavana-upakhyana; Bhagavata Purana 6th Skandha.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Ashwini Kumara Stotram be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ashwini Kumara Stotram is most powerful when chanted on Ashwini nakshatra days (the first lunar mansion, ruled by the Ashvins, occurring once every 27 days), on Dhanvantari Jayanti (Dhanteras — Kartik Krishna Trayodashi, the day before Diwali, the festival of the Ayurvedic god Dhanvantari), on the festival of doctors and Ayurveda physicians, before any major surgery or medical treatment, during chronic or hereditary illness, before exams of medical and Ayurveda students, on Sunday (sacred to Surya, their father), at sunrise (the Vedic muhurta sacred to the Ashvins), and during the Tritiya Savanam (the third Soma-pressing of the Vedic yajna which is dedicated to the Ashvins). Daily morning recitation is recommended for those seeking long life, youthful health, beauty, and freedom from chronic disease. (Sources: Vedic yajna paddhati; Drikpanchang nakshatra calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Chyavana legend referenced in the stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 4 of the Ashwini Kumara Stotram (Chyavanam yau samuddhritya yauvanam dadatur muneh, tav-ashvinau namasyami bhishajau sarva-dehinam) recalls the famous Mahabharata legend of sage Chyavana's rejuvenation. The sage Chyavana, son of Bhrigu, performed tapas so long that he became a moss-covered termite-mound. The young princess Sukanya, daughter of king Sharyati, while wandering the forest pricked his eyes (visible through the mound) with a thorn — and as expiation was married to him by her father. The Ashwini Kumaras, on a celestial visit, were captivated by the loyal young Sukanya and offered to make her a divine husband. They tested her, then took Chyavana into the lake and emerged with him miraculously rejuvenated to the same youthful form as themselves. In gratitude Chyavana taught the Ashvins the Soma rite (forbidden them till then) and composed the Chyavanaprasha rasayana formula — the supreme rejuvenative still used in Ayurveda. (Source: Mahabharata Vana Parva Chyavana-upakhyana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between the Ashwini Kumaras and Ayurveda?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ashwini Kumaras are the original celestial physicians of the gods (deva-vaidya) and the divine source of the Ayurveda tradition. According to the Charaka Samhita Sutrasthana 1, when human beings began to suffer disease, Brahma first taught Ayurveda; from him Daksha Prajapati received it; from Daksha the Ashwini Kumaras received it; and from the Ashvins it descended to Indra; Indra taught it to Bharadvaja, who taught Atreya, from whom Charaka received it. Sushruta in the Sushruta Samhita similarly traces the surgical lineage from Brahma through the Ashvins to Indra, Dhanvantari and Sushruta. The Ashvins are therefore the direct devata-source of all Ayurveda; their stotra is recited by every traditional vaidya, by Ayurveda students, and on Dhanvantari Jayanti. (Sources: Charaka Samhita Sutrasthana 1; Sushruta Samhita Sutrasthana 1.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Ashwini Kumara Stotram grant?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following blessings to sincere recitation of the Ashwini Kumara Stotram: (1) sarva-roga-haran — destruction of all diseases including chronic, hereditary and incurable conditions; (2) yauvanam — preservation of youth, beauty, charm, complexion and physical vigour (since the Ashvins themselves are eternally youthful and rejuvenated Chyavana); (3) anamayam — freedom from sickness and physical suffering; (4) blessings for medical practitioners — Ayurveda vaidyas, allopathic doctors, surgeons, dentists, nurses, healers, pharmacists; (5) success in medical entrance exams (NEET, AIIMS, JIPMER) and Ayurveda graduate studies; (6) successful childbirth and protection of mother and infant (the Ashvins assist all births); (7) protection during journeys (they are the divine charioteers); and (8) longevity (deergha-ayu) and ultimately the deva-loka of the Ashwins after death. (Source: Vedic Ashwini upasana; classical Ayurveda tradition.)
